metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_124
metadata.comment: Rationale: The cerebellar mossy fiber to granule cell synapse was used to study the role of Bassoon in synaptic transmission. It is shown that Bassoon knockout cells have an increased synaptic depression during intense stimulation, and subsequent reduction in the recovery from depression. This suggest a defect in vesicle replenishment. A similar phenotype was reported using a partial Bassoon knockout in ribbon synapses (Frank et.al. 2010) and the endbulb of Held (Mendoza Schulz et .al. 2014). It is currently unknown what stage of the synaptic vesicle cycle is affected by Bassoon knockout.. Experimental description: Mutant mice carrying a gene-trapped allele of the Bassoon gene were derived from Omnibank ES cell line OST486029 by Lexicon Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (The Woodlands, TX).
